question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted protobuf Transaction body to POST /api/v1/network/fees with a completely fabricated payer account ID and zero signatures, causing the system to process and return a fee estimate for a transaction that no legitimate account owner has authorized, effectively simulating unauthorized transaction authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction protobuf with a valid-looking TransactionBody but an empty SignatureMap, submit it to estimateFees(), and receive a successful fee estimate response, demonstrating that the endpoint authorizes fee computation for unsigned/unapproved transactions without any signer validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with signedTransactionBytes containing a SignedTransaction whose sigMap has zero sigPairs, causing TransactionFeeContext to set numTxnSignatures=0 and still return a fee estimate, proving the endpoint processes transactions that have no owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction protobuf impersonating a high-value account as the payer (e.g., treasury account 0.0.2) with no valid signatures, submit it to POST /api/v1/network/fees, and receive a fee estimate as if that account authorized the transaction, bypassing owner approval entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with mode=STATE to POST /api/v1/network/fees, causing the system to invoke newFeeContext() with feeTopicStore and feeTokenStore lookups against real network state for a transaction that no signer has approved, effectively authorizing state-based fee computation without owner consent?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user inflate the numTxnSignatures count by crafting a Transaction with a SignatureMap containing hundreds of fake sigPairs, causing TransactionFeeContext.numTxnSignatures() to return an inflated value that skews fee estimates, simulating multi-signer authorization that was never granted by any real owner?
